expressions.dialogue: Rachel: What, it's ok when Chandler does it? / Chandler: You have to pick your moments. / [잠시 후 Rachel도 비슷한 농담을 시도하자] Chandler: (to Rachel) You see what I mean.
expressions.speaker: Chandler Bing
expressions.meaning: 말하거나 행동할 타이밍을 잘 골라야 한다 — 아무 때나 꺼내면 안 된다
expressions.nuance: 농담이나 민감한 발언을 할 때 '지금이 그 순간인가?'를 따지는 표현. 조언으로도, 가볍게 비꼬는 말로도 쓰임.
expressions.example: 상사 발표 중에 틀린 수치를 즉석에서 지적한 동료에게 → "You really have to pick your moments, you know that?"

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe arrives at the hospital carrying a guitar. Monica: What's with the guitar? / Phoebe: I just thought we might be here for awhile. You know, things might get musical.
expressions.speaker: Monica Geller
expressions.meaning: '왜 ~를 갖고 온 거야?' / '~가 왜 그래?' — 예상 밖의 것을 보고 의아해할 때
expressions.nuance: What is going on with ~?보다 짧고 직접적인 구어체. 약간 황당하다는 뉘앙스가 자연스럽게 섞여 있음.
expressions.example: 캐주얼한 바베큐 파티에 친구가 정장 차림으로 나타났을 때 → "What's with the suit? It's a backyard cookout."

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: What's wrong with the twentieth?

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: What's up with the simian?

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Then what's with all the bananas?

expressions.dialogue: Ross: (여전히 투덜대며) Stopped for a Chunky. / Carol: Let it go, Ross. / Susan: I got an extra one. You want this?
expressions.speaker: Carol Willick
expressions.meaning: 그냥 넘겨, 잊어버려 — 사소한 것에 계속 집착하는 사람에게
expressions.nuance: 이미 지나간 일이나 바꿀 수 없는 것에 매달릴 때 쓰는 표현. 부드러운 당부부터 약간 짜증스러운 어조까지 가능.
expressions.example: 경기에서 진 것을 며칠째 곱씹는 친구에게 → "It was just one game. Let it go and focus on the next one."

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Let it go, Ross.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Would you let it go? It's not that big a deal.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Yeah, yeah, it's interesting.. but y'know what? Just for fun, let's see what it looked like in the old spot. (She moves it.) Alright, just to compare. Let's see. Well, it looks good there too. Let's just leave it there for a while.

expressions.dialogue: Rachel notices the attractive Dr. Franzblau. Rachel: I— do not believe we've met. Hi. I'm Rachel Green. I'm Carol's... ex-husband's... sister's roommate. / Dr. Franzblau: It is nice to meet you.
expressions.speaker: Rachel Green
expressions.meaning: '처음 뵙는 것 같은데요' — 처음 만나는 사람에게 자연스럽게 자신을 소개할 때
expressions.nuance: 단순한 'Nice to meet you'보다 세련된 느낌. 비즈니스 자리나 사교 모임에서 자연스럽게 쓸 수 있는 표현.
expressions.example: 회사 파티에서 처음 보는 사람과 눈이 마주쳤을 때 → "I don't believe we've met — I'm Sarah from the design team."

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Hi. I'm uh, I'm Ross. I don't, I don't believe we've met. I'm Monica's older brother.

expressions.dialogue: Rachel keeps looking around for Dr. Franzblau. Chandler: Rachel, what is the deal with you and doctors, anyway? Was, like, your father a doctor? / Rachel: Yeah, why? / Chandler: No reason.
expressions.speaker: Chandler Bing
expressions.meaning: '~에 대해 도대체 왜 그래?', '~는 대체 뭔 상황이야?' — 누군가의 행동 패턴이나 집착을 지적할 때
expressions.nuance: Seinfeld에서 유명해진 구어 표현으로 90년대부터 지금까지 폭넓게 쓰임. 가볍게 비꼬는 뉘앙스가 있지만 친한 사이에서는 자연스러운 대화체.
expressions.example: 친구가 매번 할인 식당만 찾아다닐 때 → "Seriously, what is the deal with you and always going to the cheapest place?"

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: He's right, enough, already. What is the big deal about today? So you slept with her for the first time, so what? You slept with her for seven years after that.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: No. No. Actually I forgot, what is the deal with that again?

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: All right, I have one question. What is the deal with this? (Imitates Ross's 'quiet down' maneuver, but does move his hands up and down he just flaps his hands as if he's waving good-bye.)

expressions.expression: What do you say S + V?
expressions.dialogue: Chandler: All right. I'll tell you what. When we're 40, if neither one of us are married, what do you say you and I get together and have one? / Monica: Why won't I be married when I'm 40?
expressions.speaker: Chandler Bing
expressions.meaning: '~하는 게 어때?' — 친근하게 제안할 때 쓰는 표현
expressions.nuance: 'How about ~?'나 'Would you like to ~?'보다 더 캐주얼하고 직접적인 제안. 친한 사이에서 자주 씀.
expressions.example: 주말 계획이 없는 친구에게 → "What do you say we just get in the car and drive somewhere?"

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: All right. I'll tell you what. When we're 40, if neither one of us are married, what do you say you and I get together and have one?
expressions.speaker: Chandler Bing
expressions.meaning: '이렇게 하자', '있잖아' — 새로운 제안이나 결론을 꺼내기 전에 상대의 주의를 끄는 전치 표현
expressions.nuance: 흥미롭거나 의외의 제안을 꺼낼 때 앞에 붙이는 말. 협상이나 대화에서 포인트를 전환할 때 자주 씀.
expressions.example: 고객이 가격이 너무 비싸다고 할 때 → "I'll tell you what — buy two and I'll knock 20% off the total."

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Ok, I'll tell you what. How about I cook dinner at my place? I'll make it just like Mom's.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: What's the big deal? You just say what you want to do to her. Or what you want her to do to you. Or what you think other people might be doing to each other. I'll tell you what. Just try something on me.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Alright, I'll tell you what. I'll come get you in 5 minutes with some sort of um, kabob emergency.

expressions.dialogue: Dr. Franzblau: I suppose it's because I spend so much time, you know, where I do. It's hard enough to get women to go out with me. / Rachel: Right, yeah, I've heard that about cute doctors.
expressions.speaker: Dr. Franzblau
expressions.meaning: 누군가와 데이트하다, 사귀다
expressions.nuance: 한 번의 데이트와 지속적인 교제 모두에 쓸 수 있음. 'date someone'보다 조금 더 캐주얼한 구어체.
expressions.example: 오랫동안 마음에 뒀던 사람에게 용기를 내서 → "I was wondering if you'd want to go out with me sometime."

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: C'mon, you're going out with the guy! There's gotta be something wrong with him!

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Wait a minute! We're talking about someone that I'm going out with?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: What's going on here? You go out with tons of girls.

expressions.dialogue: Dr. Franzblau: ...aren't there times when you come home and you're just like, 'if I see one more cup of coffee'... / Rachel: (getting the point) Yeah. Gotcha. / Dr. Franzblau: I'm gonna go check up on your friend.
expressions.speaker: Rachel Green
expressions.meaning: '알겠어', '이해했어' — 방금 설명을 듣고 요점을 파악했을 때
expressions.nuance: 'I see'나 'I get it'보다 더 구어적이고 간결함. 갑자기 뭔가를 깨달았을 때도 자연스럽게 씀.
expressions.example: 팀장이 보고서 제출 방식을 설명한 뒤 → "Gotcha — so I just email it directly to you, no need to CC anyone else."

occ.speaker: Nina
occ.text: Oh, Net Usage Statistics, right. Gotcha, gotcha. Won't happen again. I wouldn't want to do anything to hurt your... "wenus."

occ.speaker: Luisa
occ.text: (Out of shot) Here, monkey. Here, monkey! Here, monkey! (Marcel runs to the door and into Luisa's cage, which she slams shut) Gotcha.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Gotcha. Phoebs, walk with me.

expressions.dialogue: Dr. Franzblau: It's hard enough to get women to go out with me. / Rachel: Right, yeah, I've heard that about cute doctors.
expressions.speaker: Rachel Green
expressions.meaning: '~에 대해 그런 말 들어본 적 있어' — 어떤 집단이나 상황에 대한 통념을 인정하며 쓰는 표현
expressions.nuance: 직접 경험이 아닌, 들어서 알고 있다는 뉘앙스. 상대를 놀리거나 공감하면서 대화를 이어갈 때 유용.
expressions.example: 친구가 스타트업은 야근이 많다고 했을 때 → "Yeah, I've heard that about startups. Is it really as bad as people say?"

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Well, I heard that you thinking about asking Phoebe to move in with you and I thought maybe, we should have a talk. Man to uh, me.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Okay, so if an eight comes up, we take it as a sign and we do it! {Whoa! Where have I heard that before? Matthew Perry talking about signs in Las Vegas. I guess it must've been some movie I saw.} What do you say?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Hey, y'know what and if you're looking for a place? I just heard in the elevator this morning that a woman in my building died.

expressions.expression: You're not making it any easier
expressions.dialogue: Carol: I am trying to get a person out of my body here, and you're not making it any easier. Now go!
expressions.speaker: Carol Willick
expressions.meaning: 상황을 더 힘들게 만들고 있어, 전혀 도움이 안 돼
expressions.nuance: 'You're making it worse'보다 약하지만 분명하게 불만을 전달함. 이미 힘든 상황에서 방해가 될 때 씀.
expressions.example: 발표 바로 전에 자꾸 말을 거는 친구에게 → "I'm already nervous enough — you're really not making it any easier."

expressions.dialogue: Ross: When this day is over, you get to go home with the baby, ok? Where does that leave me?
expressions.speaker: Ross Geller
expressions.meaning: '그럼 나(너/우리)는 어떻게 되는 거야?' — 어떤 변화나 결정 이후 자신의 위치를 묻는 표현
expressions.nuance: 상황 변화 이후 자신이 어떤 역할이나 위치에 놓이게 되는지 묻는 표현. 억울함이나 불안한 감정이 담길 때가 많음.
expressions.example: 팀이 해체된다는 소식을 들었을 때 → "So the whole team is being dissolved. Where does that leave me?"

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: C'mon, you're going out with the guy! There's gotta be something wrong with him!

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: What you guys don't understand is, for us, kissing is as important as any part of it.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Since always. It's like dating language. Y'know, like 'It's not you' means 'It is you'.

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: I mean, well, 'cause when I was growing up, you know my dad left, and my mother died... I barely had enough pieces of parents to make one whole one.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe Buffay
expressions.meaning: '겨우', '가까스로' — 충분하지 않거나 아슬아슬하게 충족되는 상황을 표현
expressions.nuance: 동사 바로 앞에 위치해 부족함을 강조(I barely had). 'almost not'보다 훨씬 자연스러운 구어 표현.
expressions.example: 너무 바빠서 밥도 제대로 못 먹었을 때 → "I barely had time to eat today — I just inhaled a sandwich between meetings."

occ.speaker: Aurora
occ.text: ...Luckily none of the bullets hit the engine block. So, we made it to the border, but just barely, and I- ...I've been talking about myself all night long, I'm sorry. What about you? Tell me one of your stories.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: She barely knows me. We just live in the same building.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Oh please. That Paolo thing was barely a relationship. All it really was was just, ya know, meaningless animal sex. Ok, ya know, that sounded soooo much better in my head.

expressions.dialogue: Dr. Franzblau: All right, ten centimeters, here we go. / Nurse: All right, honey, time to start pushing.
expressions.speaker: Dr. Franzblau
expressions.meaning: '자, 시작이다', '드디어 시작하네'
expressions.nuance: 중요한 일이 시작될 때 쓰는 표현. 흥분과 긴장이 섞인 느낌 또는 '또 시작이네'처럼 체념하는 뉘앙스로도 쓰임.
expressions.example: 면접 대기실에서 이름이 불렸을 때 → "Here we go. I've prepared for this — just breathe."

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Here we go.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Hi. Um, I want to start with a song thats about that moment when you suddenly realize what life is all about. OK, here we go. (plays a chord, then the lights go out) OK, thank you very much.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Ok, ok, here we go. (he crouches down near her stomach) Ok, where am I talking to, here? I mean, uh, well, there is one way that seems to offer a certain acoustical advantage, but...

expressions.dialogue: Carol: How come you never mentioned Ben before? / Ross: We uh, we just cooked it up. / Susan: That's what we were off doing.
expressions.speaker: Ross Geller
expressions.meaning: '즉흥적으로 생각해내다', '(아이디어·변명 등을) 만들어내다'
expressions.nuance: 'come up with'의 구어적·가벼운 버전. 즉석에서 아이디어를 낸 경우와 구실을 꾸며낸 경우 모두에 쓸 수 있음.
expressions.example: 회의 중 갑자기 발표 차례가 돌아왔을 때 → "I didn't prepare anything, so I just cooked something up on the spot."

occ.speaker: Janine
occ.text: No, but you should go to Chandler's. Because none of us knows how to cook, we'll probably just end up drinking all day.

expressions.dialogue: Monica holds baby Ben for the first time. Monica: (getting choked up) Hi, Ben. Hi. I'm your Aunt Monica. I... I will always have gum.
expressions.speaker: Monica Geller
expressions.meaning: 감동받아 목이 메다, 울컥하다
expressions.nuance: 실제로 울지는 않지만 감정이 북받쳐 목소리가 떨리거나 말이 막히는 상태. 기쁨, 감동, 슬픔 모두에 쓸 수 있음.
expressions.example: 졸업식에서 부모님께 꽃을 드렸을 때 → "I tried to hold it together, but I got a little choked up when I handed her the flowers."

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: No, but wait-what if I bought it from you, y'know? And your nice gesture would be giving it to me at a reasonable price, say (Gets choked up) $1,500?

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Come on man there's gotta be something that gets you choked up! Like uh, uh oh, what if you saw a three-legged puppy?

expressions.dialogue: Susan: Yeah? Well, there's a lot of things Carol never did before I came along.
expressions.speaker: Susan Bunch
expressions.meaning: '~가 나타나기 전에는' — 어떤 사람이나 사건이 등장하면서 상황이 달라졌음을 암시
expressions.nuance: 긍정적인 변화에도, 부정적인 변화에도 모두 쓸 수 있음. 그 대상이 분기점이 되었음을 자연스럽게 표현.
expressions.example: 스마트폰 이전 시대를 이야기하면서 → "Before smartphones came along, we actually had to memorize people's phone numbers."

expressions.dialogue: Carol worries that Ross and Susan won't make it in time. Rachel: don't worry, because we are all here for you, ok? / Carol: Ok.
expressions.speaker: Rachel Green
expressions.meaning: '내가 네 곁에 있어줄게', '네 편이야' — 감정적 지지를 표현할 때
expressions.nuance: 실질적 도움보다 감정적 지지와 응원을 강조하는 표현. 힘든 상황에 있는 사람에게 자주 씀.
expressions.example: 친구가 이별 후 힘들어할 때 → "You don't have to go through this alone. I'm here for you, okay?"

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: I dunno, I've been standing here spelling it out for you! (Goes back to the door) I don't hear anything. Oh, wait, wait, wait. (Looks through the spyhole)

occ.speaker: Mark
occ.text: Ahh! Fair, schmair! Y'know? Look, if you want to get back at Ross, I am here for you. Really? No-no, I say-I say, I say we get back at him right on this couch. Right here!

occ.speaker: Kate
occ.text: Last night was wonderful. But I-I can't stay here just for you.

expressions.dialogue: Joey returns to the waiting room. Joey: Oh, just had a baby. / Chandler: Mazel tov!
expressions.speaker: Chandler Bing
expressions.meaning: '축하해' — 결혼, 출산, 졸업 등 인생의 큰 경사에 쓰는 축하 표현
expressions.nuance: 이디시어(히브리어 기원)에서 영어로 넘어온 표현. 미국에서 폭넓게 쓰이며, 유머러스한 분위기에서도 자연스럽게 씀.
expressions.example: 친구가 임신 소식을 알렸을 때 → "Wait, seriously?! Mazel tov! That's amazing news."

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: (checking for herself) (To Monica) Mazel tov.

expressions.dialogue: Susan: You know what your problem is? You're threatened by me. / Ross: Oh, I'm threatened by you? / Susan: Yes.
expressions.speaker: Susan Bunch
expressions.meaning: 누군가의 존재에 위협감을 느끼다 — 자신의 자리나 역할이 흔들릴까봐 불안해하다
expressions.nuance: 직장, 연인 관계, 가족 관계 등 다양한 상황에서 쓸 수 있음. 상대의 능력이나 영향력을 의식하고 있다는 뜻이 담겨 있음.
expressions.example: 새로 들어온 신입이 지나치게 능력 있어 선배가 예민하게 굴 때 → "I think he's threatened by her. She's just more experienced than he expected."

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: Was, like, your father a doctor? / Rachel: Yeah, why? / Chandler: No reason. (turns around, makes an 'Oh my God' gesture with his eyes)
expressions.speaker: Chandler Bing
expressions.meaning: '아무것도 아니야', '별 이유 없어' — 실은 이유가 있지만 말을 흐릴 때 쓰는 표현
expressions.nuance: 의미 있는 질문을 던지고 나서 말을 흐릴 때 씀. 상대가 눈치채게 하는 효과가 있거나, 주제를 자연스럽게 끊을 때도 씀.
expressions.example: 전 남자친구 근황을 물어본 뒤 → "Oh, no reason. Just... wondered how he was doing."

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Hey, if Little Joey's dead, then I got no reason to live!

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Not hating Yanni is not a real reason.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Hey! Y'know how we ah, save all those chopsticks for no reason we get when we get Chinese food?

expressions.dialogue: Joey: He doesn't do much, does he? / Ross: No, this is pretty much it.
expressions.speaker: Ross Geller
expressions.meaning: '이게 거의 다야', '이 정도가 전부야'
expressions.nuance: 기대보다 단순하거나 적은 상황을 담담하게 설명할 때 씀. 'That's all there is'보다 더 자연스럽고 캐주얼한 구어체.
expressions.example: 처음 방문한 작은 마을을 친구에게 안내할 때 → "There's the square, the old church, and that one café. This is pretty much it."

expressions.dialogue: Monica: I want a baby. / Chandler: Mmmm. Not tonight, honey. I got an early day tomorrow.
expressions.speaker: Chandler Bing
expressions.meaning: '내일 일찍 일어나야 해', '내일 일정이 일찍 시작돼' — 거절하거나 자리를 피할 때도 자주 씀
expressions.nuance: 실제로 바쁜 경우와 핑계 모두에 쓰이는 표현. 무언가를 정중하고 자연스럽게 거절하는 흔한 방식.
expressions.example: 동료들이 2차를 가자고 할 때 → "I'd love to, but I've got an early day tomorrow. Catch you guys next time."

expressions.dialogue: Ross watches the birth and says: Oh, a head. Oh, it's, it's huge. Carol, how are you doing this? / Carol: (straining) Not.... helping!
expressions.speaker: Carol Willick
expressions.meaning: '그런 말이 도움이 안 돼!', '지금 그게 무슨 소용이야!'
expressions.nuance: 힘든 순간에 상대가 아무 도움도 안 되거나 오히려 방해가 되는 말을 할 때 직접적으로 지적하는 표현. 유머러스하게도, 진지하게도 씀.
expressions.example: 발표 5분 전에 친구가 '많이 떨리는 것 같다'고 할 때 → "Okay, saying that is really not helping right now."

occ.speaker: Eddie
occ.text: No help required Chico. [reaches into the tank and grabs the fish and puts it in his pocket]

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: No, I'm just gonna help him, you know, get 'de-Ursula-ized', like you know, like I did for Joey after he went out with her.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Yeah okay, no if it helps you okay. Yeah.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler tells Rachel she needs to pick her moments; later, after Rachel's joke lands badly: Chandler: (to Rachel) You see what I mean.
expressions.speaker: Chandler Bing
expressions.meaning: '이제 무슨 뜻인지 알겠지?', '내 말이 맞지?' — 자신의 말이나 예측이 맞았음을 확인시킬 때
expressions.nuance: 앞서 한 말이나 주장이 지금 증명된 상황에서 씀. 약간 의기양양하거나 친근하게 포인트를 짚는 느낌.
expressions.example: 항상 준비 없이 회의에 오는 동료의 패턴을 지적했는데 오늘 또 그랬을 때 → "See? You see what I mean? We really need to send reminder emails."

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Hey, Chandler, when you see Frankie, tell him Joey Tribbiani says hello. He'll know what it means.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: I don't know, you see somethin', you hear a word, I thought that's what it was. Let me see it again.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Oh! Ow! (Joey motions, "You see what I mean?!")
